name: Las Lagunas de Paniagua desde Belvís de la Jara — circular por Alcaudete de la Jara
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Belvís De La Jara traverse a region characterized by its natural landscapes and dedicated cycling infrastructure. The area features the Vía Verde de la Jara, a former railway line offering a unique experience through tunnels and viaducts. Riders can also explore tranquil views of the Tagus River and the Azután Reservoir, set amidst granite and slate formations. This comarca within the province of Toledo provides varied terrain for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Belvís De La Jara The most popular mountain bike trail is Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route incorporates sections of the scenic Vía Verde de la Jara. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Santa Quiteria Station – Puente Azután Viewpoint loop from Calera-Chozas, a difficult 65.1 miles (104.8 km) path. This extensive route offers panoramic vistas around the Azután Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Azután Reservoir and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Belvís De La Jara is defined by the Vía Verde de la Jara, views of the Tagus River, and the Azután Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Belvís De La Jara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Belvís De La Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Belvís De La Jara?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Belvís De La Jara, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes, 26 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Belvís De La Jara?
A: The terrain around Belvís De La Jara is varied, featuring natural landscapes with granite and slate formations. Many routes incorporate sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346909911/aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-and-via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, which is built on a former railway line and includes both asphalt and tamped gravel sections, as well as impressive tunnels and viaducts.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346909911/aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-and-via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, with its relatively flat sections and former railway path, often provides a good option for a more relaxed ride, though specific family-focused routes should be checked for suitability.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can enjoy tranquil views of the Tagus River and the Azután Reservoir. Specific landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3851007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amador Bridge or Azután Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3853975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huso River Valley and Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Vía Verde de la Jara.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Belvís De La Jara?
A: The best times to visit for mountain biking are during &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. The weather during these seasons is generally ideal, offering comfortable temperatures for riding and showcasing the region's natural beauty, especially in spring when fields of flowers bloom.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023054626/azutan-reservoir-and-via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azután Reservoir and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.2 km circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350497559/las-lagunas-de-paniagua-desde-belvis-de-la-jara-circular-por-alcaudete-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Paniagua desde Belvís de la Jara — circular por Alcaudete de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 36.6 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Belvís De La Jara?
A: The mountain bike trails around Belvís De La Jara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Tagus River and Azután Reservoir, and the unique experience of riding through the tunnels and viaducts of the Vía Verde de la Jara.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Quiteria Station – Puente Azután Viewpoint loop from Calera-Chozas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 104.8 km path. This extensive route offers panoramic vistas and a demanding ride around the Azután Reservoir.

Q: Can I access the Vía Verde de la Jara from Belvís De La Jara?
A: Belvís De La Jara is situated within the comarca of La Jara, making it an excellent starting point for accessing the Vía Verde de la Jara. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346909911/aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-and-via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, directly incorporate sections of this scenic greenway.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that start directly from Belvís De La Jara?
A: Yes, several routes either start directly from or pass through Belvís De La Jara. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346901301/belvis-de-la-jara-to-alcaudete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de la Jara to Alcaudete&lt;/a&gt; trail, which covers nearly 30 km.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Quiteria Station – Puente Azután Viewpoint loop from Calera-Chozas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans an impressive 104.8 km.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The area is known for its tranquil and natural views, particularly along the Tagus River and around the Azután Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot offering panoramic vistas.
tips: "Belvís" means "beautiful view" perhaps referring to the beautiful landscape that dominates from its hills. The most interesting building is the Iglesia Parroquial de San Andrés Apostol, in Mudejar style.

These two small lagoons are located halfway between Buenasbodas and Alcaudete de la Jara. These are mainly fields and trails. The lagoons are an interesting place to visit, especially if you are a bird watcher.

Alcaudete de la Jara is a municipality located in the province of Toledo, in the autonomous community of Castilla-La Mancha. This charming town is located in the region of La Jara and is known for its natural surroundings and rich history.

Alcaudete de la Jara offers a peaceful atmosphere and is ideal for those who enjoy nature and outdoor activities, such as hiking and cycling.
